description: Add missing plugin settings documentation to configuration reference and CLI reference pages, including config fields, CLI commands, and example updates.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add missing config fields to configuration reference
userStories.description: Add documentation for the localPluginDirs, settings, and additionalAllowedDirectories fields in the config reference page. Also update the example config JSON to include settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Config fields table includes localPluginDirs with type string[], description mentioning parent directory auto-scanning
Config fields table includes settings with type Record<string, Record<string, unknown>>, description mentioning per-plugin settings keyed by plugin short name
Config fields table includes additionalAllowedDirectories with type string[], description mentioning extending default allowed directories for local plugins
Example config JSON block includes a settings field example showing at least one plugin with a setting value
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx (add fields to table, add field details section, update example)

Context:
The config.json schema is defined in platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts. The OpentabsConfig interface has these fields: localPlugins, localPluginDirs, permissions, settings, additionalAllowedDirectories, version. The KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS set is: ['version', 'localPlugins', 'localPluginDirs', 'permissions', 'settings', 'additionalAllowedDirectories']. The docs currently only document localPlugins, permissions, and port — missing localPluginDirs, settings, and additionalAllowedDirectories.

Changes:
1. In the 'Config fields' table (starts around line 19), add three new rows after the existing fields:
   - localPluginDirs: string[], default [], description: 'Parent directories whose immediate children are auto-scanned for plugins. Each child directory that contains a valid plugin is treated as a local plugin.'
   - settings: Record<string, Record<string, unknown>>, default {}, description: 'Per-plugin settings values. Keys are plugin short names. Values are key-value maps matching the plugin\'s configSchema. See plugin settings below.'
   - additionalAllowedDirectories: string[], default [], description: 'Extra directories where local plugins may reside, extending the default allowed roots (home directory, temp directory, current working directory).'
2. In the 'Example config' section (around line 52), add a settings field to the example JSON, e.g.:
   ```json
   "settings": {
     "jira": {
       "instanceUrl": { "production": "https://mycompany.atlassian.net" }
     }
   }
   ```
3. Add a new '### settings' field details section after the permissions section, briefly explaining that plugin settings store user-provided values for plugins that declare a configSchema, and that url-type settings are stored as Record<string, string> maps (instance name to URL) enabling multi-instance plugins. Mention the two CLI interfaces: `opentabs plugin configure <name>` (interactive) and `opentabs config set setting.<plugin>.<key> <value>` (non-interactive).
4. Do NOT document the `version` field — it's internal.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove or modify the existing port field documentation — it is correct as-is (CLI-side field).
- Keep the same table formatting style used for existing fields.
- Do NOT add a full guide on multi-instance plugins — just mention that url-type settings support it.

userStories.title: Add missing plugin configure and settings commands to CLI reference
userStories.description: Add documentation for opentabs plugin configure, opentabs config set setting.<plugin>.<key>, and opentabs-plugin readme commands. Fix the plugin subcommand count.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs plugin section intro says 'six subcommands' (not 'five')
opentabs plugin configure <name> has its own subsection with description, argument table, and examples
config set key format table includes setting.<plugin>.<key> row with description
opentabs-plugin readme command has its own subsection with --dry-run, --check options
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx (add configure subcommand, update count, add readme command, add settings key format)

Context:
The CLI reference (cli.mdx) documents five plugin subcommands but `opentabs plugin configure <name>` exists per platform/cli/CLAUDE.md. It interactively prompts for each configSchema field, validates values, and saves to config.json under settings.<pluginShortName>. Also, `opentabs config set setting.<plugin>.<key> <value>` is a non-interactive way to set a single setting. The opentabs-plugin readme command generates a README.md from the built manifest.

Changes:
1. Line 354: Change 'Has five subcommands: search, list, install, remove, and create.' to 'Has six subcommands: search, list, install, remove, create, and configure.'
2. Add a new '### opentabs plugin configure' subsection after the 'opentabs plugin create' section (before 'opentabs plugin list'). Content:
   - Description: Interactive command that prompts for each field defined in the plugin's configSchema. Shows current value as default. Validates URL fields, select fields, boolean fields, and number fields. Saves to config.json and notifies the server.
   - Argument table: <name> (required) — Plugin name (short name, e.g., 'jira').
   - Example: `opentabs plugin configure jira`
3. In the 'opentabs config set' key format table (around line 631), add a new row:
   - Key: `setting.<plugin>.<key>`, Value: any, Description: 'Set a plugin setting value. Pass an empty string to remove the key.'
4. In the 'Plugin Developer CLI' section at the bottom, add a new '### opentabs-plugin readme' subsection after 'opentabs-plugin inspect'. Document:
   - Description: Generate a user-facing README.md from dist/tools.json and package.json.
   - Options: --dry-run (print to stdout), --check (exit 1 if README.md is out of date)
   - Examples: `opentabs-plugin readme`, `opentabs-plugin readme --check`

Constraints:
- Match existing formatting patterns (Table components, code blocks, etc.).
- Do NOT reorder existing subcommand sections.
